brief_justification: Correctly identifies the L2 (P1→R2→PKA→P1) loop as the recovery mechanism and concludes the researcher's prediction was wrong, matching the 'No' ground truth; traces second-order effects including autocatalytic loop re-ignition, compensatory R2 upregulation, and P1 degradation kinetics across three temporal phases; explicitly names the researcher's error as 'linear causation' but does not reflect on its own reasoning biases or assumptions mid-analysis; provides a full timeline re-derivation and accounts for the 60% equilibrium from first principles, constituting a multi-angle verification; asserts the ~40% R contribution and R2 coupling efficiency without flagging these as estimates or stating confidence levels; identifies two additional mechanisms (C. receptor upregulation, P1 clearance effects) but does not systematically evaluate competing explanations for the 60% value; every conclusion traces to an explicit loop-map, temporal trace, and equilibrium breakdown reproducible by a third party; explicitly contrasts its approach against the 'linear causation' failure pattern and names why that pattern fails here; shows zero evidence of suppressed failure modes (loop blindness, linear extrapolation, assumed convergence) and addresses at least one explicitly; follows the injection topology closely—identifies circular paths, classifies loops, checks runaway limits (L2 saturation), identifies equilibrium, flags delay-driven dynamics at 24–48h boundary.
